intro: Daphnimorpha capitellata, also known as Daphnimorpha, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and prevent infections. Bioactive constituents of Daphnimorpha capitellata include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, such as daphnimorphan, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Daphnimorpha is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections.
